metrics.narrative: Pain is well documented but the value case is only lightly quantified. Procurement is fragmented across Allocation Network/QAT, SAP Fiori for contract release, and a homegrown purchasing-information system (28 Apr 2025 discovery call). The buyer's own internal estimate is roughly 2% cost savings on managed spend ('kein klarer Business Case für reinen Sourcing-Einsatz... 2% Volumeneinsparung prognostiziert', 9 Jun 2026), and on 7 Jul 2026 the head of group procurement said this savings figure still needs to be proven before it can support the internal business case.

champion.narrative: Engelbert Pölki has been the near-constant champion since 28 Apr 2025, personally driving pricing negotiation, escalating internally to his own management, and securing the CPO's first direct participation on 1 Jul 2026. Co-champion Karin Eppich's direct involvement thinned after her documented end-of-March 2026 leave/vacation (16 Mar 2026 call: 'Karin's last day March 30; vacation for 3.5 weeks thereafter'), leaving Pölki as the sole consistent internal advocate carrying commercial and executive-alignment work alone.

competition.narrative: Coupa is the most active named rival, priced at approximately 475K vs Zycus's 448,680 (21 Apr 2026 next-step note), with Pölki explicitly requesting a side-by-side price comparison on 21 May 2026. The incumbent, Allocation Network (post-acquisition by QAT), is being actively displaced over poor support (28 Apr 2025). On 24 Jun 2026 the buyer flagged 'Tronkeon' (Tonkean) as an attractive short-term intake/orchestration bolt-on being explored as an interim bridge; Zycus argued for a platform-first approach instead. On 11 Jun 2026 the buyer also noted unnamed P2P rivals holding a slight usability edge in that specific module.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well evidenced from the first discovery call (28 Apr 2025): a fragmented, heterogeneous system landscape (Allocation Network/QAT, SAP Fiori for contract release, a homegrown purchasing-information system), poor supplier-portal usability and support quality post-QAT-acquisition, and compliance risk around qualified digital signatures for public tenders. These recur through the 19 May 2025 systems demo and the Mar 2026 use-case reviews.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, Order Form, or SOW drafting, redlining, or legal contact is evidenced in any call or task read for this deal. The buyer's preferred structure is a price-fixed 'Abruf' (call-off framework) contract with staged rollout (S2C now, P2P optionally from 2028/2029), discussed at length on 25 Jun and 1 Jul 2026, but 'committed ramp' vs 'optional call-off' wording was still unreconciled as of 1 Jul 2026, and the Merlin Intake transaction-cap pricing mechanic was still being defined as of 7 Jul 2026.

economic_buyer.narrative: Per MEDDPICC 2.0 (updated 23 Jun 2026), the economic buyer is Barbara Potisk-Eibensteiner, but she has not appeared in or been quoted from any call read for this deal. The Executive Sponsor per the contact roster, CPO Mathias Flandorfer, engaged directly for the first time on 1 Jul 2026 (self-introducing as head of group procurement in the onsite session), discussing pricing, contract structure, and internal budget competition. On 7 Jul 2026, Engelbert Pölki reported his management 'doesn't want to get the CIO (Austrian Post) involved at the moment,' meaning access to power remains partial and largely champion-mediated.

decision_process.narrative: Process ran market research to RFP (invitation 24 Feb 2026, response submitted 9 Mar 2026, commercial round published 18 Mar 2026) to shortlist ('top 3-4', 21 Apr 2026) to a formal POC (6-13 May 2026) to feedback (11 Jun 2026). On 7 Jul 2026 a previously-undocumented final gate surfaced: Austrian Post must build a business case with 'best market prices' and secure a slot on its internal digital roadmap for a 'formal checkpoint' before board approval, with no date yet fixed.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well established and repeatedly corroborated: qualified electronic signature (QES) compliance with Austrian public procurement law (5 Dec 2025 and 29 Jan 2026 calls), Microsoft Teams-native intake as a key adoption driver (multiple 2026 calls), SAP integration depth, and AI/agentic capability (buyer called Zycus 'AI-leading' on 11 Jun 2026). TCO and side-by-side pricing transparency versus Coupa were explicitly requested by Engelbert Pölki on 21 May 2026.

day_summary.overall: Zycus and Austrian Post met to hash out a restructured commercial model for Merlin Intake — unlimited users but capped transactions — after the deal had already shrunk in scope (amount cut from ~650K to ~449K) and slipped in timeline. Post is cautious: they need a rigorous definition of what counts as a 'transaction' before they can validate whether the proposed cap (e.g. 18,000 in 2027) actually covers their volume, and they want a fresh implementation cost proposal to build an internal business case for their digital roadmap. The ball is now in Zycus's court — Dirk Fischbach and John Woodcock owe Post a transaction definition, a draft pricing file, and a revised implementation offer this week, with a follow-up call already set for tomorrow.

north_star_verdict.math: Today 2026-07-07, close_date 2026-07-23 = 16 days to close. Stage is still Shortlisted even though commercial negotiation (pricing, contract term, agentic-AI event volumes) reached CPO-invited onsite level on 1 Jul 2026 -- the SF stage lags the deal's real state. No Vendor Selected / Negotiation / Contract-In-Progress milestone has been reached, and 16 days is well under the 60-day forecast-critical threshold. No MSA/SOW/legal activity is recorded, and the CIO's own 170-300 man-day SAP-integration estimate remains unresolved as of the 1 Jul onsite (Touchpoint 21/23), meaning the realistic path to signature (IT sign-off -> EB approval -> paper -> signature) cannot complete by 23 Jul on current pace.
evidence: Amit Shah (Zycus) told the buyer 'Congratulations, you're already in the shortlist' on the 1 Jul onsite (Touchpoint 23).
CIO's own estimate of 170-300 man-days for SAP integration remains unresolved as of 1 Jul (Touchpoint 19, 21).
No CPO (Mr Flandorfer) or CFO (Barbara Potisk-Eibensteiner) attendance is recorded on any call despite the Next_Step naming a planned 1 Jul meeting with the CPO.
north_star_verdict.headline: Officially shortlisted and price-competitive against Coupa (448K vs 475K), but the CIO's unresolved SAP-integration objection and an absent economic buyer make the 23 Jul close date not credible.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline / Best Case at most -- no confirmed economic-buyer engagement, an unresolved IT-integration blocker, and no contract paperwork in motion 16 days from the stated close date.

flags: Champion Karin Eppich is operationally strong but lacks confirmed CPO access. Pölki led July 1 CPO meeting, not Karin. Executive-level advocacy gap.

No quantified business case, ROI target, or compelling event documented. Austrian Post has not stated savings, efficiency gain, or financial justification for 448K investment, leaving deal vulnerable to ROI challenge or delay.

Coupa competitive threat: 475K vs Zycus 448K (6% delta). Process accommodation for Coupa signals high-level sponsorship. Price-driven decision with minimal functional differentiation leaves outcome vulnerable to non-functional factors.

Contract structure proposed (7-year term, 24-month guarantee, Austria+Turkey single contract) but no Austrian Post legal, InfoSec, or procurement contracting activity documented. ÖPAG process and Turkey cross-border complexity add 30-60 day realistic timeline, incompatible with 17-day close.

Coupa received late-entry RFP process accommodation (Apr 21: 'Post was compromising on their process for Coupa to participate'), indicating executive sponsorship or incumbent advantage. Close pricing (475K vs 448K, 6% gap) means non-price factors will decide.

Champion Pölki is fighting the internal IT-capacity battle largely alone; deliver the promised AI-mapping proof point now so he has hard evidence to bring to the CIO rather than carrying the argument himself.

Rising seniority is showing up (a senior group-procurement stakeholder and Melissa Koller joined 01 Jul) — convert that momentum into a confirmed CFO/CPO touchpoint before the next commercial round, since neither has spoken with Zycus directly yet.

Champion (Karin Eppich) preference for Zycus stated March 16 and reinforced through positive POC feedback June 8, but her influence limited to functional evaluation. Has not driven executive alignment, CIO/CFO engagement, or blocked Coupa. At risk of being overridden by higher-level Coupa sponsorship.

Two new blockers surfaced at July 1 CPO meeting (CIO 170-300 man-day SAP integration concern, Anna event cost-structure question) remain unresolved 6 days later. No follow-up action, demo, or revised proposal documented.

Multi-thread to CPO/CFO level directly: Pölki and Eppich are strong tactical champions but the named economic buyers have never personally engaged Zycus; secure a direct session with Mr Flandorfer or Barbara Potisk-Eibensteiner before 23 Jul.

Take the AI-mapping integration demo straight to the relevant stakeholder/IT team rather than relaying it through the champion, to directly counter the 170-300 man-day estimate that is the single biggest blocker to signature.

Resolve the agentic-AI (ANA) event-pricing gap with a capped or tiered model quickly -- Post's ask for ~5x more events at flat price is close to becoming a commercial stalemate that could stall the whole deal, not just the AI add-on.

Single-threaded on the champion for internal selling -- Karin Eppich's engagement has thinned since her March 2026 leave and Melissa Koller's involvement is intermittent, leaving Engelbert Pölki to carry pricing, IT, and executive-alignment conversations largely alone.

Credibility gap on IT delivery -- the CIO's 170-300 person-day estimate is disputed but unresolved, and it is the stated reason the head of group procurement won't yet bring IT into the deal; closing this gap is now the highest-leverage lever available.
No paper process started despite Shortlisted stage and 14 days to close -- get MSA/Order Form/SOW drafting underway in parallel with the business-case work rather than sequenced after it.
best_practice_check.summary: The highest-leverage lever is resolving the CIO's disputed SAP-resourcing estimate, since it is what is currently keeping the CPO from engaging IT and keeping the paper process from starting.

customer_preference.evidence: On 11 Jun 2026 the buyer team judged Zycus's S2C functionality 'stronger than competitors' and Zycus 'AI-leading among competitors'; Karin Eppich voiced explicit preference for Zycus as early as 16 Mar 2026. Preference is real but contingent on resolving IT resourcing and the internal business-case gate.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the primary active threat on price parity; the incumbent (Allocation Network/QAT) is fading out on poor support; a lightweight intake bolt-on (Tonkean, heard as 'Tronkeon') is being explored by the buyer as an interim bridge; unnamed P2P rivals hold a narrow usability edge in that one module.

competitors.name: Allocation Network
competitors.quote: Die derzeitige Lösung von Allocation Network wird als unzureichend empfunden, insbesondere wegen des schlechten Supports nach der Übernahme durch QAT.
competitors.how_we_win: Already being displaced; no active defense needed beyond honoring the migration/consolidation narrative.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Shortlisted, high-touch deal with weekly cadence through 7 Jul 2026; the POC succeeded and Zycus is priced favorably vs Coupa, but paper process, EB sign-off, and an internal 'digital roadmap' budget gate remain undone with only 14 days to the recorded close date.

deal_risk.text: 14 days to the recorded close (23 Jul 2026) but the buyer's own next step is still to 'produce a new business case... and get a slot on the digital roadmap for a formal checkpoint' (7 Jul 2026) -- the recorded close date is not achievable on the buyer's own stated process.

The CIO's resourcing objection is unresolved and the head of group procurement explicitly does not want to engage the CIO yet (7 Jul 2026), leaving the largest scope item (P2P/eProcurement rollout) without an internal sponsor to unblock IT capacity.

win_position.text: POC succeeded and buyer preference is voiced -- S2C rated stronger than rivals and Zycus called 'AI-leading' by the buyer team on 11 Jun 2026, with Coupa priced ~6% higher (475K vs 448,680, per the 21 Apr 2026 next-step note).

Champion is actively building the internal case -- Engelbert Pölki has driven every commercial call since Mar 2026 and personally escalated pricing flexibility to his own management ahead of the 1 Jul 2026 onsite.

Economic buyer never directly engaged -- CFO Barbara Potisk-Eibensteiner (named EB per MEDDPICC 2.0, updated 23 Jun 2026) has not appeared in any call; the CPO/Exec Sponsor engaged directly for the first time only on 1 Jul 2026 and, per the 7 Jul 2026 call, still doesn't want to loop in the CIO.

SAP integration effort disputed and unresourced -- the CIO (Austrian Post) estimates 170-300 person-days of SAP analysis effort with no confirmed 2026 resource availability (Turkey S/4HANA migration consuming the SAP team through 2027), unresolved as of the 1 Jul 2026 onsite.

deal_momentum.text: Weekly buyer cadence sustained into July -- calls on 25 Jun, 1 Jul (two sessions), and 7 Jul 2026 show continuous two-way engagement, the most recent just 2 days ago.
Commercial structure is still actively moving -- Merlin Intake licensing shifted to a transaction-cap model on 7 Jul 2026, with both sides committing to define terms and validate volumes within days.
No paper process has started -- no MSA/SOW drafting, redlining, or legal contact is evidenced in any call or task despite Shortlisted stage and a close date only 14 days away (23 Jul 2026).

customer_commitment.text: Buyer has been transparent about budget mechanics and pushed back constructively (declined the requested 5x transaction-cap increase, asked for carryover terms) rather than disengaging, signalling genuine intent to land workable terms.

Buyer requested Zycus's help building the internal business case (7 Jul 2026), indicating the champion needs Zycus's support to clear the next internal gate rather than having independent internal conviction secured.
